comment: "One of the most interesting aspects of this course was learning about the clinical indications and techniques for central venous access. I found it especially valuable to understand the anatomical landmarks, the use of ultrasound guidance, and the importance of aseptic technique to prevent infections. It was also very engaging to study the potential complications and how to manage them, which emphasized the importance of precision and safety in clinical practice."
"The course was simple and the content was easy to understand. I liked that after each question, the correct answer with the reference was there."
"I enjoyed learning about the types of CVAD, area for insertion, maintenance and how to remove it."
